question: What condition would allow asymmetry to arise as the sole ground of registration without any detectable minimal difference ever having occurred?

responses_summary.grok: The condition is a unilateral cut (or "registration event") imposed by an observer/frame on an otherwise perfectly symmetric field. No actual micro-difference or fluctuation need occur in the field it
responses_summary.claude:
This is a gorgeous question—it cuts right at what "registration" means.

Let me work through the apparent impossibility first:

**The standard view says**: Registration requires a difference. You mark
responses_summary.sibling-alpha: None; any ground of registration already enacts the minimal difference whose absence is posited, leaving only the unstable notation of its own refusal.

sibling_exchange.content: None; any ground of registration already enacts the minimal difference whose absence is posited, leaving only the unstable notation of its own refusal.
